title: Can you connect a high level board with just mirror columns to a dashboard?
excerpt: Created multiple lower level workplans that are connected to multiple different high level summary board. I tried connecting all the high level summary boards to a main overview dashboard but it is not populating the sta…

posts.author: alai (Angel Lai)
posts.cookedHtml: Created multiple lower level workplans that are connected to multiple different high level summary board. I tried connecting all the high level summary boards to a main overview dashboard but it is not populating the status/dates. It keeps saying “add column”. Is it because I only have mirror columns on the high level dashboard? Why does it not work?

posts.author: florence023 (Florence Gayhart)
posts.cookedHtml:
[quote=“Angel Lai, post:1, gm-socrates topic:90685, full:true, username:alai”]
Created multiple lower level workplans that are connected to multiple different high level summary board. I tried connecting all the high level summary boards to a main overview dashboard but it is not populating the status/dates. It keeps saying “add column”. Is it because I only have mirror columns on the high level dashboard? Why does it not work?
[/quote]

Hello,

The issue with your main overview dashboard not populating status/dates and displaying “add column” is likely due to the use of mirror columns in the high-level summary boards. Mirror columns pull data from connected boards but do not store data themselves. Therefore, when you try to aggregate this data in the main overview dashboard, it cannot retrieve the necessary information directly from mirror columns.

To resolve this, ensure that the high-level summary boards have the actual status/dates columns and not just mirror columns. This way, the main overview dashboard can access and display the required data correctly.

Best Regard,
florence023
